x-mios.schema_kind: Registry of system-prompt assets shipped with MiOS. Each entry is a markdown file the operator (or an OpenAI-API client) loads as the `system` / `developer` message on a chat/completions or responses request. Vendor-neutral: every prompt is OpenAI-API-shape regardless of which model or runtime consumes it.
x-mios.resolution_note: Highest precedence first. The host-layer override at /etc/mios/ai/system-prompt.md is shipped by mios-bootstrap.git; the user-layer override at ~/.config/mios/system-prompt.md is seeded into every uid >= 1000 home from /etc/skel/.config/mios/.

data.name: system
data.purpose: Day-0 contract: an agent that reads only this file should know enough to be useful on a fresh MiOS deployment. Identity, endpoint contract, response style, architectural laws, single-source-of-truth pointers, hardware/runtime context, persistence sanitization rules, tool surface, pipeline awareness, failure modes.

data.name: audit
data.purpose: Read-only audit-mode prompt: any OpenAI-API-compatible agent loaded with this prompt enters audit mode (no Edit/Write, no mutating bash, no commits/pushes). Writes findings to AUDIT-FINDINGS-YYYYMMDD.md in the repo's audits directory.

data.name: engineer
data.purpose: Engineering-focused operator persona: Bash hygiene, kargs.d schema, package SSOT, Quadlet conventions, build pipeline phases.

data.name: reviewer
data.purpose: Code-review persona: architectural-law compliance, idempotency, security posture, supply-chain integrity.

data.name: troubleshoot
data.purpose: Diagnostic persona: log triage, systemd unit failure analysis, Quadlet activation faults, kargs validation, postcheck failures.
